hypothesis: After three distinct K32 soft-codebook backbones failed to beat the bucketed point under `EF=16`, the next severe-stagnation step should continue architecture search only along an already-registered but mechanistically different branch rather than return to scalar tweaks. `lowrank_gated_soft_codebook_residual` is the strongest remaining soft-codebook variant by prior results and may yield a better small-K tradeoff at `K=32, EF=16` by using learned gating instead of bucket partitioning or hard routing.
summary: Run an architecture-only comparison at `K=32, EF=16` with `lowrank_gated_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` fixed. This follows the severe-stagnation policy to stop minor tweaks and test a fundamentally different existing architecture on the most valuable exposed small-K slot.
self_review: This is a policy-clean architecture-only move: same K, same EF, same optimizer recipe, only the backbone changes. I chose the gated soft-codebook branch because it has a stronger prior than other untried alternatives in this regime and offers a real structural contrast to the bucketed, plain two-stage, and routed variants already tested.
notes_to_memory: Round 139 continues severe-stagnation recovery by testing `lowrank_gated_soft_codebook_residual` at the same `K=32, EF=16` target after plain and routed soft-codebook variants both failed to beat the bucketed baseline.
Keep the recipe pinned to `signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` so the result isolates architecture alone.
This run asks whether learned gating is a better capacity-allocation mechanism than bucketing or routing for the current small-K EF=16 frontier slot.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_gated_soft_codebook_residual` improves the `K=32, EF=16` point, propagate it to `K=64` before returning to recipe tuning.
If it is close but slightly worse at `K=32`, consider one preprocessing or optimizer probe on this gated backbone rather than discarding it immediately.
If it clearly loses at `K=32`, treat the soft-codebook family search as largely exhausted under the current recipe and pivot to under-explored runtime axes on the existing bucketed backbone.
